text.title: personas Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for personas as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Too many personas (8+) that the team cannot remember.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each additional persona dilutes focus. Beyond 5 primary + 2 secondary, teams default to "it applies to everyone" and personas become wallpaper.

failure-mode.description: Personas without scenarios are pretty posters, ignored in real decisions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Abstract goals feel detached. Scenarios (Sarah's Monday 9am rush) make the persona operational — designers test features against the scenario, not the persona name.

failure-mode.description: LLM-generated personas inherit dataset biases; they look real but reproduce stereotypes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents synthesize plausible-sounding personas from averages. Require quote-with-participant-ID for every trait; reject invented demographic attributes.

failure-mode.description: Creating personas before research — "made-up" personas reinforce assumptions and push the team in the wrong direction.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pre-research personas become self-fulfilling prophecies. Teams design to confirm, not discover. Wait for evidence.

failure-mode.description: Personas that are just demographics (age, gender, income) with no goals or behaviors.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Demographics don't predict behavior. A 35-year-old female manager and a 35-year-old female engineer have nothing in common. Goals and behaviors are the decision-making levers.

failure-mode.description: Photos generated by AI; introduces demographic bias and accessibility issues.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: AI-generated faces encode dataset biases and can misrepresent marginalized groups. Use illustrations or initials instead.

failure-mode.description: Agents paraphrasing quotes silently; personas become agent interpretations, not evidence.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Paraphrasing loses nuance and lets agents inject their own biases. Every trait must have a verbatim quote with participant ID.

failure-mode.description: Agents inventing demographic attributes that weren't in source data.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs synthesize plausible-sounding attributes. If the research says "Sarah is a manager" but doesn't mention income or family status, the agent shouldn't invent it. Stick to documented traits.

failure-mode.description: Agents producing 8+ personas if not capped explicitly.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unconstrained agents maximize coverage. Enforce max-cluster constraint and reject low-frequency clusters.

failure-mode.description: Re-clustering on a different week produces different personas; no locking of corpus snapshot.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Different data → different clusters. Lock the interview corpus used; timestamp the clustering. If you re-cluster, note it as a refresh, not a redo.
